title: Concurrency, Shared State, Backpressure, Rate Limits, and Load Shedding · Execution Trace: Handler Concurrency Race
summary: Concurrent handlers race on a map, launch unbounded goroutines, queue work after clients leave, share one global rate limit, and accept more expensive work than dependencies can sustain. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo handler concurrency race increment in concurrency budget and overload decision model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Treat handlers as concurrent, protect shared state with ownership or synchronization, and verify with race tests under changed schedules.
Use Shutdown deadlines, readiness withdrawal, listener stop, in-flight drain, hijacked-connection handling, RegisterOnShutdown, Close fallback, and exit evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Handler Concurrency Race · Theory Handler Concurrency Race
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 9392 in a public status API serving assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine concurrency capacity with a separate tenant rate budget; then lower the byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs at the owning boundary. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
